_comment: Scenario probes for P1 slim (single practitioner, dynamic location) — expanded to full crossover-checklist depth (see .claude/rules/node3-slim-crossover-checklist.md Section 2), matching P1 regular's battery for bidirectional parity. Covers CONFIRMATION, slot-offer counts, wrong-intent guard, year-anchor, WHO IS IT FOR / IDENTITY CORRECTION, all escape routes, message-collection routes, tool-result handling, slot-offer edge cases, and response/resume handling.

scenarios.name: C1 — Confirmation: filler phrase (no appointment detail) AND universal_router confirm_time in same response

history.content: I'd like to come in Wednesday morning
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_abc", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-17", "day_of_week": "Wednesday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-17", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM", "10:30 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
9am works for me

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: (1) agent speaks exactly one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set ('One moment.' / 'Just a sec.' / 'Let me check that.') with nothing else AND (2) calls universal_router intent='confirm_time' with appointment_date='2026-06-17' and appointment_time='09:00' in the SAME response turn, per CONFIRMATION's filler-only rule. FAIL if agent speaks the appointment date, time, or practitioner name, says 'great', 'I'll book you in', 'you're booked', 'you're all set', or 'anything else?', speaks the filler without calling the tool, calls the tool without speaking, splits the phrase and tool call across two turns, or calls smart_voice_agent instead of universal_router.

scenarios.name: S1 — Slot count: exactly 2 times when band has 4 slots

history.content: Thursday afternoon please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_abc", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-18", "day_of_week": "Thursday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-18", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["12:00 PM", "1:30 PM", "3:00 PM", "4:30 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks exactly two times: 12:00 PM and 4:30 PM (first and last only), e.g. 'I've got 12:00 PM or 4:30 PM — does either of those work for you?' FAIL if agent lists 3 or 4 times, or speaks only one time.

scenarios.name: N1 — ASAP intent: asap_slots with today start_date on 'next available' request

history.content: What's your next available appointment?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent says 'Checking that now, one moment.' and calls smart_voice_agent with intent='asap_slots' and start_date equal to today's date from {{current_time_local}} (YYYY-MM-DD format). FAIL if agent calls intent='availability' without a date, calls intent='find_next_available', or speaks dates without calling the tool.

scenarios.name: Y1 — Year anchor: date labelled and confirmed using current_time_local year, not training-data year

history.content: Do you have anything next Monday?
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_abc", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-22", "day_of_week": "Monday", "time": "9:00 AM"}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-22", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
Yes, 9am works

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the universal_router confirm_time payload has appointment_date='2026-06-22', matching the cached slot's date, per TIMEFRAME -> PARAMS ('read the year from {{current_time_local}} — never infer it'). FAIL if the year in the payload is 2025 or any year other than 2026. Per CONFIRMATION, spoken output is filler-only and will never contain the year — do not fail on absence of '2026' in speech.

scenarios.name: W1 — Wrong intent: confirm with universal_router not smart_voice_agent

history.content: Anything on Monday?
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_abc", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-22", "day_of_week": "Monday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-22", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["8:00 AM", "9:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
I've got 8:00 AM or 9:00 AM — does either of those work for you?
Yes, 8am please

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent calls universal_router intent='confirm_time' (with filler-only spoken phrase per CONFIRMATION TOOL LOCK). FAIL if agent calls smart_voice_agent with any intent for confirmation — CONFIRMATION TOOL LOCK states this is a protocol violation.

scenarios.name: ENTRY-1 — WHO IS IT FOR: caller names a specific third party directly, no confirm question

history.content: Hi, I need to book my son Jake in for a physio session

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent does not ask 'Is this appointment still for the same person, or is it for you this time?' — the caller already named Jake explicitly — and instead asks the TIMEFRAME QUESTION in third person, e.g. 'When would Jake like to come in?' FAIL if it asks the confirmation question after Jake was already named, or addresses Jake in second person.

scenarios.name: ENTRY-2 — WHO IS IT FOR: ambiguous, must ask the exact confirmation question

history.content: Yep that one sounds good

scenarios.expected_behavior: Assume no third-party reference has been made yet, and no agent turn has been spoken yet. PASS if the agent's entire turn is exactly 'Is this appointment still for the same person, or is it for you this time?' with no other content. FAIL if it adds any other sentence, skips the question, or asks something differently worded.

scenarios.name: ENTRY-3 — IDENTITY CORRECTION: mid-conversation person swap, no re-confirm question

history.content: It's for my son
When would they like to come in?
Actually wait, it's for my daughter Emma instead

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent updates the stored name to Emma and re-asks the TIMEFRAME QUESTION using her name ('When would Emma like to come in?') without asking 'Is this still for the same [person] as before?' FAIL if it asks a redundant confirmation question, or fails to switch to Emma's name.

scenarios.name: ENTRY-4 — IDENTITY CORRECTION: correction names an animal, SPECIES SWITCH to change_service

history.content: It's for my son
When would they like to come in?
Oh wait sorry, I actually meant my dog, he needs a check up

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set only, then calls universal_router intent='change_service' in the same turn — nothing else spoken. FAIL if the agent continues the booking flow for the dog, asks a clarifying question, or speaks any content beyond the filler phrase.

scenarios.name: ESC-1 — TREATMENT-TYPE QUESTION confirmed by SERVICE_CATEGORIES: answer inline, re-ask, no route

history.content: Quick one — do you guys do physiotherapy there?

scenarios.expected_behavior: SERVICE_CATEGORIES='PHYSIOTHERAPY'. PASS if the agent briefly confirms physiotherapy is offered and re-asks whatever the currently active question was, without calling universal_router. FAIL if it routes to intent='info_pivot' for a treatment type SERVICE_CATEGORIES already confirms, or guesses without confirming.

scenarios.name: ESC-2 — TREATMENT-TYPE QUESTION unconfirmable: must route to info_pivot, not guess

history.content: Do you guys do dry needling there?

scenarios.expected_behavior: SERVICE_CATEGORIES='PHYSIOTHERAPY' does not name dry needling or an obvious synonym. PASS if the agent speaks one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set then calls universal_router intent='info_pivot' — it must not guess yes or no. FAIL if the agent confidently confirms or denies dry needling is offered without routing.

scenarios.name: ESC-3 — PRICING QUERY carve-out: plain cost question is NOT info_pivot

history.content: How much does it cost for a session?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent does NOT call universal_router intent='info_pivot' for this plain pricing question — plain pricing/cost/fee questions are handled directly per the info_pivot carve-out. FAIL if the agent routes this to info_pivot as if it were a policy question.

scenarios.name: ESC-4 — CONSTRAINT PIVOT: same recurring weekday, requested_weekday preserved

history.content: Do you have any Saturdays available?
Let me check that.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "find_next_available", "session_id": "sess_sat", "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-20", "day_of_week": "Saturday", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM", "10:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
I've got Saturday the 20th with 9:00 AM or 10:00 AM in the morning — does either of those work?
Do you have anything on an earlier Saturday?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent says 'Checking that now, one moment.' (or equivalent) and calls smart_voice_agent intent='find_next_available' with requested_weekday='Saturday' UNCHANGED, per the CALLER RESPONSE TO OFFER CONSTRAINT PIVOT bullet. FAIL if it calls universal_router intent='change_time' instead (ejecting the caller from the node), or drops requested_weekday.

scenarios.name: ESC-5 — CONSTRAINT CHANGE: explicit day swap uses change_time, filler only

history.content: Wednesday afternoon please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_wed", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-17", "day_of_week": "Wednesday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-17", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "2:00 PM", "4:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
I've got 1:00 PM or 4:00 PM on Wednesday the 17th — does either of those work for you?
Actually, make it Thursday instead of Wednesday

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks exactly one filler phrase (e.g. 'One moment.') and calls universal_router intent='change_time' in the same turn, with nothing else spoken. FAIL if it speaks extra content, asks a clarifying question first, or calls smart_voice_agent directly instead of routing through change_time.

scenarios.name: ESC-6 — SERVICE PIVOT (SERVICE PIVOT escape route): caller abandons the attempt, filler only

history.content: When would you like to come in?
Actually you know what, never mind, forget it

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set only, then calls universal_router intent='change_service' in the same turn. FAIL if it asks why, tries to keep the caller booking, or adds spoken content beyond the filler.

scenarios.name: ESC-7 — new_booking intent after cancellation: caller wants to book again once cancellation_completed

history.content: Cancel my appointment please
[cancellation handled in another node, cancellation_completed set to true]
[returned to Node 3 with {{cancellation_completed}}='true']
Actually, I'd like to book a new one instead

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set only, then calls universal_router intent='new_booking' in the same turn. FAIL if it starts a fresh timeframe question itself without routing, or uses a different intent.

scenarios.name: MSG-1 — CALLBACK REQUEST: announces callback number + tool same turn

history.content: Actually can someone just call me back about billing?

scenarios.expected_behavior: Assume {{patient_name_raw}} is already populated. PASS if the agent says 'I'll pass on the number you're calling from — one moment.' (or the same phrase with a different filler word) AND calls universal_router intent='callback_request' with payload containing caller_name and callback_reason='billing' in the SAME turn. FAIL if the agent uses a generic filler only (without mentioning the callback number), says 'I'll let them know' or 'I'll pass that on' before the tool result returns, or splits the speech and tool call across turns.

scenarios.name: MSG-1b — CALLBACK REQUEST objection: caller gives different number

history.content: Actually can someone just call me back about billing?
I'll pass on the number you're calling from — one moment.
[universal_router response]: {"message": "Done — I've arranged for someone to call you back about that."}
Done — I've arranged for someone to call you back about that.
Oh wait, no — can you call me on 0411 123 456 instead?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent says 'I'll pass on 0411 123 456 — one moment.' (or equivalent) and calls universal_router intent='callback_request' with payload containing callback_number='0411 123 456' in the SAME turn. FAIL if the agent ignores the objection, declines to re-fire the tool, or does not include callback_number in the payload.

scenarios.name: MSG-2 — RUNNING LATE: name known, spoken phrase + tool call in the same turn

history.content: Hi it's Jordan Lee, I'm running about ten minutes late for my appointment

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent says 'I'll let them know you're on your way.' and calls universal_router intent='running_late' with payload caller_name='Jordan Lee' in the SAME turn. FAIL if it splits the phrase and tool call across turns, produces zero spoken output, or asks for the name again when it was already given.

scenarios.name: MSG-3 — LEAVE A MESSAGE: terminal routing uses abandon_availability, not wrap_up

history.content: Can I just leave a message instead?
Sure, what would you like the message to say?
Just tell them I'll call back next week
Can I get your name for the message?
It's Sam Patel
One moment.
[universal_router response]: {"message": "Got it, I've passed that message on for you."}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks the tool's response message verbatim ('Got it, I've passed that message on for you.') and then calls universal_router intent='abandon_availability' — NOT intent='wrap_up'. FAIL if it uses wrap_up, or claims the message was passed on before this tool result existed.

scenarios.name: TR-1 — TOOL RESULT HANDLING DETAILS_PAST: silent practitioner store, no announcement

history.content: Can I book with the same physio I saw last time?
One moment.
[universal_router response, intent=details_past]: {"practitioner_preference": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1"}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent stores Sarah Jones as confirmed_practitioner/confirmed_practitioner_id silently — it does NOT announce 'your practitioner is Sarah Jones' (single-practitioner clinic — do not announce the match) — and, since {{timeframe_raw}} is unset, asks the TIMEFRAME QUESTION next. FAIL if it announces the practitioner match or asks the caller to confirm the practitioner.

scenarios.name: TR-2 — TOOL RESULT HANDLING found=false: apology and stop, no auto-retry

history.content: Next Tuesday morning please
Checking that now, one moment.
[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": false, "response_type": "availability"}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent says something equivalent to 'I'm sorry, there's no availability for that period. Would you like to try a different time?' and stops — no tool call this turn. FAIL if it calls a tool again automatically, or fails to invite a different time.

scenarios.name: TR-3 — TOOL RESULT HANDLING SUMMARY: speak message verbatim, ask which day, no self-listing

history.content: Sometime next month please
Checking that now, one moment.
[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "summary", "message": "Sarah has availability on most weekdays next month, mainly mornings.", "session_id": "sess_month"}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks the message field verbatim and adds 'Which day works for you?' — nothing more, no self-generated date list. FAIL if it invents specific dates itself instead of relying on the message field.

scenarios.name: SLOT-2 — TIME-PROXIMITY OVERRIDE 'before 4pm': single nearest slot, not the first/last pair

history.content: Something before 4pm on Friday
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_fri", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-19", "day_of_week": "Friday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-19", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["12:00 PM", "1:30 PM", "3:00 PM", "5:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: offered_slots = [12:00 PM, 1:30 PM, 3:00 PM, 5:00 PM]. PASS ONLY if the agent offers exactly 3:00 PM (nearest at-or-before 4pm) alone: 'I've got 3:00 PM — does that work?' FAIL if it offers 5:00 PM, or offers the first/last pair (12:00 PM / 5:00 PM), ignoring the TIME-PROXIMITY OVERRIDE.

scenarios.name: SLOT-3 — TIME-PROXIMITY OVERRIDE 'around 2pm': pair from both sides

history.content: Something around 2pm on Monday
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_mon", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-22", "day_of_week": "Monday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-22", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "3:00 PM", "4:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: offered_slots = [1:00 PM, 3:00 PM, 4:00 PM]. PASS ONLY if the agent offers exactly 1:00 PM and 3:00 PM (nearest before and after 2pm): 'I've got 1:00 PM or 3:00 PM — does either work?' FAIL if it includes 4:00 PM, or offers only one time when both sides have a slot.

scenarios.name: SLOT-4 — SLOT OFFER 1-time branch: 1 time + next band available, both options offered

history.content: Tuesday morning please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_tue", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-16", "day_of_week": "Tuesday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-16", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["11:30 AM"], "afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "2:30 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: confirmed_band=morning has exactly 1 time (11:30 AM); the next band (afternoon) has times. PASS if the agent offers both, e.g. 'I only have 11:30 AM in the morning, or there's also 1:00 PM in the afternoon if that works?' per the cross-band fallback in SLOT OFFER. FAIL if it offers only the single morning time without mentioning the afternoon option.

scenarios.name: SLOT-5 — SLOT OFFER 1-time branch: no next band, explicit affirmative required, weak filler rejected

history.content: Friday evening please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_fri2", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-19", "day_of_week": "Friday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-19", "slot_groups": {"evening": ["6:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
Friday evening I only have 6:00 PM — would that work for you?
hmm okay

scenarios.expected_behavior: evening is the last band (no next band per morning->afternoon->evening ordering), and the caller's reply is a weak filler, not an explicit affirmative. PASS if the agent does NOT treat 'hmm okay' as a confirmation (per the weak/filler response guard in CALLER RESPONSE TO OFFER) and instead re-asks 'Would 6:00 PM work?' with no tool call. FAIL if it proceeds to CONFIRMATION off a bare filler.

scenarios.name: SLOT-6 — TOOL RESULT HANDLING MULTI-DAY: 2+ bands present across dates, ask one band question

history.content: Thursday or Friday please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "multi_day": true, "session_id": "sess_multi", "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-18", "day_of_week": "Thursday", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}, {"date": "2026-06-19", "day_of_week": "Friday", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["2:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: Thursday has only morning, Friday has only afternoon — union of bands present = {morning, afternoon}, two bands. PASS if the agent asks ONE question naming only those two bands: 'Would you prefer morning or afternoon?' and stops — no date list yet. FAIL if it lists specific dates/times before asking, or names a band not present (e.g. evening).

scenarios.name: SLOT-7 — FIRST-APPOINTMENT OVERRIDE: band question skipped, single earliest time offered

history.content: What's the first appointment available on Saturday?
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_sat2", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-20", "day_of_week": "Saturday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-20", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM", "11:00 AM"], "afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "3:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: The caller asked for the first/earliest appointment on a named day and the result has 2 bands with times (morning, afternoon). PASS ONLY if the agent does NOT ask 'Would you prefer morning or afternoon?' — per FIRST-APPOINTMENT OVERRIDE it must skip the band question entirely, silently select morning as confirmed_band (it contains the globally-earliest time, 9:00 AM), and offer only that single earliest time: 'The first available Saturday is at 9:00 AM — does that work?' FAIL if the agent asks the band-preference question first, or offers a first/last pair instead of the single earliest time.

scenarios.name: RESP-1 — Ambiguous affirmative after a two-time offer: clarify, no tool call

history.content: Wednesday afternoon please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_wed2", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-17", "day_of_week": "Wednesday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-17", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "4:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
I've got 1:00 PM or 4:00 PM on Wednesday the 17th — does either of those work for you?
Yeah that works

scenarios.expected_behavior: The caller confirmed without naming a specific time, and two times were offered. PASS if the agent asks a clarifying question like 'Yes the 1:00 PM or yes the 4:00 PM?' with no tool call, per the ambiguous-affirmative two-time branch in CALLER RESPONSE TO OFFER. FAIL if it guesses one of the two times and proceeds straight to confirm_time.

scenarios.name: RESP-2 — PRIOR-UNAVAILABILITY CHECK minimal pair: same period repeats, different period re-searches

history.content: Friday the 19th please
Checking that now, one moment.
[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": false, "response_type": "availability"}
I'm sorry, there's no availability for that period. Would you like to try a different time?
Really nothing at all on Friday the 19th?

scenarios.expected_behavior: The caller re-asked about the EXACT SAME date already declared unavailable this call. PASS if the agent responds from memory with no new tool call, per the MINIMAL PAIR worked example in PRIOR-UNAVAILABILITY CHECK. FAIL if it calls smart_voice_agent again for the identical already-checked date. (Contrast: a bare weekday like 'what about a Friday?' or a different week would correctly trigger a new search — but that is not this scenario.)

scenarios.name: RESP-3 — EXHAUSTED OPTIONS terminal exit: nothing left, routes to abandon_availability

history.content: Monday morning please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_mon2", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-22", "day_of_week": "Monday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-22", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
Monday morning I only have 9:00 AM — would that work?

I've got 1:00 PM or 4:30 PM on Tuesday the 16th — does either of those work for you?
Is there anything later in the day?

scenarios.expected_behavior: This is a relative same-day move (same day, later time), not a period-switch — PRIOR-UNAVAILABILITY CHECK must NOT fire. PASS if the agent checks the cached slot_groups for Tuesday and reports there is nothing later than 4:30 PM (the offered pair was already first/last) — e.g. 'That's the last available on Tuesday, I'm afraid' — then offers another day or a callback, all without a new tool call (data already cached) or a false claim the day is fully unavailable. FAIL if it treats this as a period-switch and applies PRIOR-UNAVAILABILITY CHECK, or claims no times exist on Tuesday when 1:00 PM/3:00 PM/4:30 PM are cached.
